qas.q: Official colors of the Marine Corps
qas.a: Scarlet and Gold

qas.q: Immediate Action for a Weapon Jam
qas.a: Tap. Rack. Bang.

qas.q: Remedial Action for a Weapon Jam
qas.a: SPORTS: Seek Cover, Pull Charging Handle, Observe Round or Brass Ejecting, Release Bolt, Tap Forward Assist, Sight and Fire Weapon

qas.q: Four Safety Rules for All Weapons Systems
qas.a: Treat,Never,Keep,Keep: 1.Treat every weapon as if it were loaded. 2.Never point your weapon at anything you do not intend to shoot. 3.Keep your finger straight and off the trigger until you are ready to fire. 4.Keep the weapon on safe until you intend to fire.
Magazine Inserted, Round in Chamber, Bolt Forward, Safety On, and Ejection Port Closed
Not Applicable with M16A2
Magazine Inserted, Chamber Empty, Bolt Forward, Safety On, Ejection Port Closed
No Magazine, Chamber Empty, Bolt Forward, Safety On, Ejection Port Closed

NATO 5.56mm

qas.q: M16A2 Maximum Effective Range on a Point Target
qas.a: 550 Meters

qas.q: M16A2 Maximum Effective Range on an Area Target
qas.a: 800 Meters

qas.q: First General Order of the Guard
qas.a: To take charge of this post and all government property in view.

qas.q: Second General Order of the Guard
qas.a: To walk my post in a military manner, keeping always on the alert, observing everything that takes place within sight or hearing.

qas.q: Third General Order of the Guard
qas.a: To report all violations of orders I am instructed to enforce.

qas.q: Fourth General Order of the Guard
qas.a: To repeat all calls from posts more distant from the guardhouse than my own.

qas.q: Fifth General Order of the Guard
qas.a: To quit my post only when properly relieved.

qas.q: Sixth General Order of the Guard
qas.a: To receive, obey, and pass on to the sentry who relieves me; all orders from the commanding officer, officer of the day, and officers and non-commissioned officers of the guard only.

qas.q: Seventh General Order of the Guard
qas.a: To talk to no one except in the line of duty.

qas.q: Eighth General Order of the Guard
qas.a: To give the alarm in case of fire or disorder.

qas.q: Ninth General Order of the Guard
qas.a: To call the corporal of the guard in any case not covered by instructions.

qas.q: Tenth General Order of the Guard
qas.a: To salute all officers and all colors and standards not cased.

qas.q: Eleventh General Order of the Guard
qas.a: To be especially watchful at night and during the time for challenging, to challenge all persons on or near my post, and to allow no one to pass without proper authority.

qas.q: Four core elements of a MAGTF
qas.a: Command Ground Combat, Aviation Combat, and Combat Service Support

qas.q: What is the historical significance of the French Fourragere?
qas.a: Worn in recognition of the accomplishments at Belleau Wood, Soissons, and Blanc Mont

qas.q: Three major developments of the Korean War
qas.a: Flak Jackets, Thermal Boots, and Vertical Envelopment Doctrine
PEP: Preserve Order, Enforce Regulations, Protect Property

qas.q: What is the origin of the term "Leatherneck"?
qas.a: Marines wore high leather collars from 1775-1875.

qas.q: What is the origin of the term "Devil Dog"?
qas.a: Nickname given to Marines by Germans, "Teufelhunde", at the Battle of Belleau Wood during WWI.
The Marine Corps Band
Captain Samuel Nicholas

qas.q: The Grand Old Man of the Marine Corps
qas.a: Archibald Henderson
James F. Amos

qas.q: Current Sergeant Major of the Marine Corps
qas.a: Sergeant Major Carlton Kent

qas.q: What is the significance behind the Blood Stripe worn on NCO and Officer uniforms?
qas.a: It signifies the blood that was shed at the Battle of Chapultepec during the Mexican War.

qas.q: What is the significance behind the NCO sword?
qas.a: They are the only NCOs of any branch still authorized to carry swords.

qas.q: What is the significance of the Quatrefoil?
qas.a: It is a cross-shaped braid worn on the top of officers' barracks cover, which originally enabled sharpshooters in the riggins of sailing ships to distinguish between friend and foe.

qas.q: What is the significance behind the Mameluke Sword?
qas.a: The sword presented to Lt. Presley Neville O'Bannon by Prince Hamet for the Marines actions in the Battle of Tripoli.

qas.q: Who are the only two Marines to be awarded two Medals of Honor?
qas.a: Smedley Butler and Dan Daly

qas.q: Who is the first Marine to be awarded the Medal of Honor?
qas.a: Cpl. John F. Mackie

qas.q: Who is the only Marine to earn 5 Navy Crosses?
qas.a: Colonel Louis B. "Chesty" Puller
Lt. Alfred Cunningham

qas.q: Father of the Marine Corps Scout Sniper Program
qas.a: Gunnery Sergeant Carlos Hathcock

qas.q: Who stated, "Gone to Florida to fight the Indians. Will be back when the war is over."?
qas.a: Commandant Archibald Henderson, 1836

qas.q: Who stated, "Come on, you sons of bitches! Do you want to live forever?"?
qas.a: Gunnery Sergeant Dan Daly during the Battle of Belleau Wood, 1918

qas.q: Who stated, "Uncommon valor was a common virtue"?
qas.a: Admiral Nimitz said it about the Marines after the Battle of Iwo Jima.

qas.q: Who stated, " The raising of that flag on Suribachi means a Marine Corps for the next 500 years."?
qas.a: James Forrestal, Secretary of the Navy, Feb. 23, 1945

qas.q: Who stated, "Some people spend an entire lifetime wondering if they made a difference in the world. But, the Marines don't have that problem."?
qas.a: President Ronald Reagan, 1985

qas.q: Where was the first amphibious landing?
qas.a: New Providence, Bahamas 1776

qas.q: Five major Marine Corps Battles of WWII
qas.a: Bouganville, Iwo Jima, Guadalcanal, Okinawa, Tarawa

qas.q: What was the battle which was fought in order to provide a landing strip for strategic bombers?
qas.a: Iwo Jima

qas.q: Four major battles of the Korean War
qas.a: PICS: Pusan, Inchon, Chosin Reservoir, Seoul

qas.q: What was the battle in which Marines were held in a 77-day seige?
qas.a: Khe Sanh, Vietnam 1968

qas.q: What was the battle in which Marines from house to house and street to street?
qas.a: Hue City, Vietnam 1968
